The traditional way of doing this would have been to have that traffic be carried across those two networks by, for example, providers and others like third parties that you would have had to pay for, for example. The concept of peering is that those two networks can just directly connect to each other and allow the exchange of traffic to happen between them without going through a third party, without involving any third parties that you typically have to pay for that traffic to go through. So the idea is the two networks. can directly connect to each other by using obviously network technologies that are available to us. So they need to be strengthened. That's the concept of what we call bastion host or bastion in general, for example. It's been strengthened, it's been hardened, and so on. Thank you.
